title: Ryanair Unveils Major Summer 2026 Expansion at Glasgow Prestwick with 10 Routes

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Ryanair said on 23 February that it had launched its Summer 2026 schedule from Glasgow Prestwick with 10 routes. The programme includes holiday destinations such as Lanzarote, Palma de Mallorca and Tenerife, as well as city-break routes including Barcelona and Pisa.

sections.heading: Schedule details
sections.content: The airline said it would add frequencies on five routes for the season: Alicante, Faro, Pisa, Malaga and Murcia. Ryanair said the schedule is supported by two Glasgow Prestwick-based aircraft, representing a local investment of 200 million US dollars, and that fares were available from GBP 29.99 through its app.

sections.heading: Airport response
sections.content: Prestwick commercial director Zoe Kilpatrick said Ryanair has operated from the airport for more than 30 years and described the extra frequencies as a response to strong demand from passengers in Ayrshire and across the wider west of Scotland. She said the summer launch would give travellers greater choice and flexibility.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Ryanair Unveils Major Summer 2026 Expansion at Glasgow Prestwick with 10 Routes
